metadata.title: Breeder profile web research - July 15 batch 8 m
metadata.notes: Public-web research only; no external communication was made. Employer, breeder, applicant, agent, licensee, and legal-holder roles remain distinct. Statuses containing record_split_required prevent an aggregated name from transferring historical rights to a later employer or combining unrelated owner chains.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Jodi Neal is part of the Queensland Department of Primary Industries Australian Strawberry Breeding Program. The cited portfolio is a State of Queensland program and commercial material is distributed through authorized program channels; the records do not support personal ownership by Neal.
profiles.headquarters: Queensland Department of Primary Industries strawberry program, Nambour, Queensland, Australia
profiles.leadershipSummary: Neal worked with former program leader Mark Herrington and remains visible in the 2025 team with Katie O'Connor, Joanna Gillespie, Michelle Paynter, and Ky Mathews. The named multi-person public team provides continuity.
profiles.candidateParent: State of Queensland, Department of Primary Industries
profiles.candidateParentBasis: Official program publications identify Neal as a DPI author and breeder-team member, while program materials name the Queensland department and State program; no personal title is established.

webResearchSources.label: National Strawberry Varietal Improvement Program report
ASBP Scarlet Rose program and PBR fact sheet

profiles.webResearchNotes: Public institution and strategic breeding program; not an individual acquisition target. Keep title and licensing analysis at the State/program and cultivar level, including any Hort Innovation or Australasian Plant Genetics commercialization role.

profiles.ownershipType: private berry breeder with entity- and territory-specific rights chains
profiles.ownershipSummary: Gerrit de Weert bred the Advaber raspberry lines through De Weert Beheer B.V. with Advanced Berry Breeding. The older US Advabertwee patent was assigned to Advanced Berry Breeding; later Canadian and Swiss PBR records name Allberry B.V. and, for some rights or territories, FuelPlants B.V. The separate 2023 FPFR strawberry records with Niels Arts belong to an Advanced Berry Breeding application chain and must not be rolled into the later raspberry holders.
profiles.headquarters: Entity-specific Netherlands locations: De Weert Beheer in Rossum, Advanced Berry Breeding in De Kwakel, and Allberry/FuelPlants in Hedel
profiles.leadershipSummary: The program has a broader bench through co-breeder Andre Smaal and, in later strawberry records, Niels Arts. Institutional holders reduce sole-breeder dependency, but no successor to de Weert was publicly identified.
profiles.candidateParentBasis: The official Canadian PBR record identifies de Weert as breeder through De Weert Beheer B.V.; it does not prove his current title, equity, or ownership of rights now held by Allberry or FuelPlants.

webResearchSources.label: Canadian Advabertwee PBR holder and breeder record

profiles.webResearchNotes: Record split is mandatory by crop, cultivar, jurisdiction, and date. Verify current Dutch corporate ownership and each assignment before treating De Weert Beheer, Advanced Berry Breeding, Allberry, or FuelPlants as the economic parent.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Mary M. Calkins is a Driscoll's-affiliated strawberry inventor. Cited patents assign the DrisStraw rights to Driscoll's, Inc.; no source supports personal title in the cultivars.
profiles.headquarters: Driscoll's, Inc., 345 Westridge Drive, Watsonville, California 95076, United States
profiles.leadershipSummary: The records consistently include Philip J. Stewart, Esther J. Kibbe, and Raymond L. Jacobs III, showing a team-based corporate program with institutional continuity.
profiles.candidateParentBasis: Patent records pair Calkins as inventor with Driscoll's as assignee, but no reliable public source establishes her exact current title.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Corporate employee profile; roll the portfolio into Driscoll's and do not use consumer-relations contact details as evidence of cultivar title.

profiles.ownershipType: corporate breeding advisor with public-research predecessor history
profiles.ownershipSummary: E.J. Meulenbroek resolves to Egbertus Joseph 'Bert' Meulenbroek, currently Breeding Advisor at Fresh Forward. His 1991-1997 work belongs to the CPRO-DLO and later Plant Research International/Wageningen predecessor program; Fresh Forward was formed in 2005. Post-formation patents such as FD 1604 were assigned to Fresh Forward Holding B.V. Historical program rights must not be assigned retroactively to his later employer without a cultivar-level transfer record.
profiles.headquarters: Fresh Forward Breeding & Marketing, Hogewoerd 1C, 6851 ET Huissen, Netherlands
profiles.leadershipSummary: Meulenbroek is now an advisor within a visible team that includes product development, molecular breeding, phytopathology, operations, and cultivation staff. The institutional bench is strong despite his long tenure.

webResearchSources.label: Fresh Forward official team and portfolio

profiles.webResearchNotes: Split pre-2005 CPRO/PRI work from Fresh Forward-era rights. Current corporate employee and strategic operator; not a standalone owner target.

profiles.ownershipSummary: The Romanized name likely maps to the common Korean name Lee Ho-young (이호영), but the dashboard aggregates peach, citrus, and apple records without a unique address, entity, or identifier. The official Korean register also exposes a separately personally held persimmon record under the same Hangul name. Those records cannot be assumed to belong to one breeder or rightsholder.
profiles.leadershipSummary: No source-backed employer, company, team, successor, or operating bench was identified for the aggregated profile.

webResearchSources.label: Korea Seed and Variety Service PVP register search

profiles.webResearchNotes: Record split required. Cultivar names are registry evidence attached to the dashboard spelling, not proof of one identity. Do not assign an employer, headquarters, contact, acquisition status, or cross-crop ownership until application-level Korean records provide matching addresses or entities.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Felicidad Fernández Fernández is NIAB's Senior Fruit Breeder. The EMR 20171 and EMR 20172 rights originated in the East Malling program; the US assignment chain for EMR 20171 runs from Fernández to East Malling Services Ltd. and later to NIAB. East Malling Services is now a wholly owned NIAB subsidiary. Preserve the dated assignment chain rather than treating NIAB as the original holder at every historical point.
profiles.headquarters: NIAB at East Malling, New Road, East Malling, Kent ME19 6BJ, United Kingdom; NIAB corporate headquarters are in Cambridge
profiles.leadershipSummary: Fernández joined the East Malling site in 2001, inherited the raspberry program from Vicky Knight in 2008, and describes herself as the third generation of women raspberry breeders there. A permanent team, interns, and the East Malling Rubus Breeding Consortium provide continuity.
profiles.webResearchNotes: NIAB is the current institutional rollup and a strategic research operator. Split historical East Malling Services ownership from later NIAB assignments and verify territory-specific consortium licenses separately.

profiles.ownershipType: provincial government strawberry researcher and breeder
profiles.ownershipSummary: The crop-specific Tae Il Kim is repeatedly affiliated with the Nonsan Strawberry Experiment Station, Fruit and Vegetable Research Center, Chungcheongnam-do Agricultural Research and Extension Services. The protected varieties arise from that provincial public breeding program; the evidence does not support personal ownership by Kim.
profiles.headquarters: Chungcheongnam-do Agricultural Research and Extension Services, Strawberry Research Institute, Nonsan, Chungcheongnam-do, South Korea
profiles.leadershipSummary: Repeated co-breeders include Won Suk Jang, In Ha Lee, Myeong Hyeon Nam, Hyun Sook Kim, and Heecheol Lee. The official institute continues the program and supplies disease-free varieties, providing a substantial public-sector bench.
profiles.candidateParent: Chungcheongnam-do Agricultural Research and Extension Services, Nonsan Strawberry Experiment Station
profiles.candidateParentBasis: Peer-reviewed publications and the official institute page support the exact crop-program affiliation, but no reliable current page establishes Kim's title.

webResearchSources.label: Official Chungnam ARES and Nonsan station overview
Korea Seed and Variety Service PVP register

profiles.webResearchNotes: Public institution profile; retain Chungcheongnam-do as the program-level rightsholder and confirm holder text application by application in the Korean register.

profiles.ownershipType: private peach-program breeder with academic affiliation; legal owner unresolved
profiles.ownershipSummary: All 15 peach records pair Lee Guk Jin with Lee Youn Do. Public profiles identify Lee Youn Do as the founder or representative of Gyeongbok Breeding Nursery and tie the same DoHee, Kum Hong, Kumbi, Danpre, Bobae, and Geumjang portfolio to that nursery. A crop-matched research publication places Guk Jin Lee at Yeungnam University. No public source establishes whether the protected rights sit with either individual, the nursery, or another applicant, so the academic and private-program records must remain split.
profiles.headquarters: Gyeongbok Breeding Nursery program: Gyeongsan, Gyeongsangbuk-do, South Korea; registered holder headquarters unresolved
profiles.leadershipSummary: Lee Youn Do is the publicly identified founder and senior breeder; Lee Su Jin appears in later co-breeder records. Lee Guk Jin's role, ownership, and succession position are not publicly established.
profiles.candidateParentBasis: The repeated co-breeding relationship with founder Lee Youn Do and the exact cultivar overlap support a program affiliation, but they do not prove Lee Guk Jin's title, employment, equity, or ownership.

webResearchSources.label: Gyeongbok Breeding Nursery founder and cultivar portfolio
Korea Seed and Variety Service PVP register

profiles.webResearchNotes: Record split required. Do not score or roll up until the Korean application records reveal applicants, rightsholders, and addresses for each cultivar. The candidate-parent field is an affiliation lead only.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Maribel Martinez Negrete is named as a Driscoll's breeder in official PBR records. Driscoll's, Inc. is the applicant and holder; no source supports personal title in the varieties.
profiles.headquarters: Driscoll's, Inc., 345 Westridge Drive, Watsonville, California 95076, United States
profiles.leadershipSummary: The portfolio is produced by a recurring corporate team including Omar Carrillo Mendoza, Philip J. Stewart, Luis Miguel Rodriguez, and Jorge Rodriguez Alcazar, providing institutional continuity.
profiles.candidateParentBasis: The official Canadian PBR record explicitly identifies Martinez Negrete's Driscoll's affiliation and Driscoll's as holder, but does not state her current job title.

webResearchSources.label: DrisStrawSixtyThree PBR holder and breeder record

profiles.webResearchNotes: Corporate employee profile; roll into Driscoll's. Trade names are included only where the official PBR record states them; no trademark ownership inference is made.

profiles.ownershipType: legacy national-research pear breeder with later private-company affiliation
profiles.ownershipSummary: Toyohide Nishibata was a breeder in the Japanese national pear program now administered by NARO, and the associated pear rights remain with NARO or its government predecessors. NARO publications identify a later affiliation with Matsutani Chemical Industry Co., Ltd.; that later employer has no demonstrated title to the historical pear portfolio and must not receive it by rollup.
profiles.headquarters: Historical rights program: NARO fruit-tree research in Tsukuba, Ibaraki, Japan; later sourced employer: Matsutani Chemical Industry, Itami, Hyogo, Japan
profiles.leadershipSummary: The cultivars were developed by a large NARO team including Toshihiro Saito, Kazuo Kotobuki, Katsuyuki Suzuki, Osamu Terai, and others. Institutional ownership and the continuing NARO breeding bench reduce individual succession risk.
profiles.candidateParentBasis: NARO publication footnotes identify Nishibata's later Matsutani affiliation, without a job title or any transfer of NARO pear rights. The evidence is historical and is not confirmation of 2026 employment.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Split the historical NARO employer and rights from the later Matsutani affiliation. Public-institution portfolio; no personal ownership or current private-company title is established.

profiles.ownershipType: private-company blackberry breeder identity with separate academic history
profiles.ownershipSummary: Mexico's official gazette granted blackberry breeder-right titles 3496-3504 to BT Genetics, S.A.P.I. de C.V. for the nine 2024 applications associated with this exact breeder name in plant-variety data. Publications place Lourdes Mateos-Maces at Colegio de Postgraduados through 2020. No public source establishes her role, employment, equity, or assignment relationship with BT Genetics, so the academic identity and private-company portfolio must not be merged beyond the supported name-and-cultivar link.
profiles.headquarters: BT Genetics registered headquarters not found in reliable public sources; historical academic affiliation was in Montecillo, Texcoco, Mexico
profiles.leadershipSummary: Mateos Maces is the sole named breeder across the aggregated blackberry records. No official company website, leadership roster, or succession bench was found for BT Genetics.
profiles.candidateParentBasis: Official Mexican titles name BT Genetics as holder of Natalia, Oliva, Rosita, Rosvi, Nélida, Sara, Romana, Elsa, and Donaji, while variety data names Mateos Maces as breeder. This supports a portfolio affiliation only, not her title or ownership of the company.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Record split required. Legal title is verified to BT Genetics for the cited Mexican grants, not to Mateos Maces personally. Keep the profile unresolved for entity relationship, headquarters, leadership, contact, and any US record until primary patent or assignment documents are available.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Kensuke Fujita is associated with the Kumamoto Prefecture citrus breeding team. Official Kumamoto pages identify the prefecture and its Agricultural Research Center as developer of the Hino and Kumamoto EC cultivars; the evidence supports prefectural, not personal, ownership.
profiles.headquarters: Kumamoto Prefectural Agricultural Research Center, Fruit Tree Research Institute, Uki, Kumamoto, Japan
profiles.leadershipSummary: The records repeatedly include Masaru Sakanishi, Akira Isobe, Minoru Mitsuta, Mitsuyasu Kitamura, and other prefectural co-breeders. The multi-person government program provides continuity.
profiles.candidateParent: Kumamoto Prefectural Agricultural Research Center, Fruit Tree Research Institute
profiles.candidateParentBasis: Official prefectural pages document the exact cultivar program and development institution, while variety records name Fujita among the breeder team; no current personal title was found.

webResearchSources.label: Kumamoto governor statement on EC11 development

profiles.webResearchNotes: Public prefectural program; not an individual acquisition target. Confirm active registrations and any private licenses cultivar by cultivar.

profiles.ownershipType: public university research support specialist and breeder
profiles.ownershipSummary: The abbreviated K. Maloney resolves to Kevin Maloney of Cornell AgriTech. His older strawberry work and later apple work were both conducted within Cornell's Geneva programs; Cornell University is the assignee for the cited apple patents and manages the variety catalog and licensing.
profiles.headquarters: Cornell AgriTech, Geneva, New York, United States
profiles.leadershipSummary: Maloney moved from grape and small-fruit programs into the apple program with Susan K. Brown and remains listed by Cornell as Research Support Specialist III. Cornell's faculty, technical staff, and licensing office provide continuity.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Both crop phases remain Cornell institutional work, so no employer split is needed. Registered symbols shown in Cornell's catalog are not converted into trademark ownership fields without a mark-level registry record.

profiles.ownershipType: Yamanashi prefectural grape breeder with non-grape homonyms in aggregate
profiles.ownershipSummary: Koichi Koike is verified as a breeder in the Yamanashi Fruit Tree Experiment Station grape program, and Yamanashi Prefecture is the institutional owner for that chain. Twelve grape records share the same Yamanashi co-breeder pattern. The two pear records and one strawberry record do not share that team and cannot be assigned to the Yamanashi breeder without further identifiers.
profiles.headquarters: Yamanashi Fruit Tree Experiment Station, 1204 Ezohara, Yamanashi City, Yamanashi 405-0043, Japan
profiles.leadershipSummary: The official historical report places Koike's breeding participation in 1996-2006 within a large prefectural grape team. Later registrations reflect the institutional program, but no evidence establishes his continued employment or a personal successor.
profiles.candidateParentBasis: The official prefectural report and MAFF record place Koike in the grape breeding team, but do not establish a current title. This affiliation applies only to the matched grape cluster.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Split and suppress the pear and strawberry records pending full Japanese names, applicants, and addresses. The verified grape cluster is a non-actionable prefectural portfolio.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Frank Kappel conducted pear and sweet-cherry breeding for Agriculture and Agri-Food Canada. Historical pear patents and current cherry PBRs name Agriculture and Agri-Food Canada or His Majesty the King in Right of Canada as assignee or holder. Summerland Varieties Corp. is the Canadian agent and commercialization interface, not the owner.
profiles.headquarters: Breeding program: Summerland Research and Development Centre, Summerland, British Columbia; current Crown holder address in cited PBRs: 1341 Baseline Road, Ottawa, Ontario, Canada
profiles.leadershipSummary: Kappel retired in 2011 after 28 years. Federal institutional ownership, successor breeding staff, and Summerland Varieties Corp. agency provide continuity beyond the individual breeder.

webResearchSources.label: SPC414 Canadian PBR holder, breeder, and agent

profiles.webResearchNotes: The pear and cherry phases resolve to the same AAFC career and legal-owner family, so no record split is required. Keep Crown ownership distinct from SVC's agent and commercialization role.
